title: Handling Form State
description: Manage multiple inputs with useState and reduce boilerplate.

slides.title: Multiple Inputs Challenge 🤹
Using a Single State Object

quizData.question: Why might you use a single state object instead of multiple useState calls?

options.text: It reduces repetition and makes it easier to manage many fields

quizData.explanation: A single object groups related state, reducing boilerplate and making updates more systematic.

slides.title: Handling Submit
Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content: Consider using a library like `react-hook-form` for complex forms, but for most apps, managing state manually is sufficient and keeps dependencies low.
